summary: GH-94 implementation is approved for a version/lifecycle metadata-only PR. The package stays proposed, lock input remains declarative, and the PR does not add fixtures, post-check logic, validator behavior, registry behavior, lockfile behavior, App UI, runtime matching, or write capability.
reviewed_validation_summary: Local validation passed for `jq empty sites/example/read-public-page/manifest.json sites/example/read-public-page/lifecycle-metadata.json .loom/bootstrap/init-result.json .loom/specs/GH-94/build-evidence.json`, `git diff --check`, `loom fact-chain --target . --json`, `loom suite validate --target . --item GH-94 --json`, and `loom suite carrier validate --target . --item GH-94 --json` at reviewed implementation head `229d5f81aef041fdf8abf8b5abbc4ab9981281cf`. `loom build --target . --item GH-94 --build-evidence .loom/specs/GH-94/build-evidence.json --json` was classified as a Loom build adapter gap after direct suite and carrier validation passed.
follow_ups: GH-95 must add the redacted fixture before the package can become fixture-backed.
GH-97 and GH-98 must add post-check and failure mapping before validator/Core consumption Work Items can close later FRs.
GH-99 through GH-103 must cover local package resolution, package ref/lock, sample package closeout, Core fixture consumption, and deferred write guardrail.

semantic_review_disposition.reason: GH-94 adds only package-local lifecycle/version metadata and matching carrier evidence. It preserves the existing package identity, keeps lifecycle proposed, and excludes lockfile, registry, runtime, App UI, and write behavior.
